summary: Added a `-d`/dump mode to `build.sh` that, after building, regenerates map data with `tools/parse_map.py` and runs `dadosod` on `build/ssbm.us.1.2/main.dol` to populate a local `dump/` directory. When combined with `-e`, the generated dump is also synchronized into `expected/dump`. The PR also added `dump/` to `.gitignore` so generated disassembly output is not committed.

key_files.reason: Introduced the `dump` flag, command-line usage text, getopts handling, post-build dump generation, optional syncing to expected, and preservation of the build exit status.

what_changed: Extended the usage string from `[-hcefmnsxl] [-r pattern]` to include `-d`.
Documented `-d` as dumping the built DOL to asm and noted its dependency on Rust plus `cargo install --git https://github.com/InusualZ/dadosod`.
Added `d` to the `getopts` option string and mapped it to `dump=true`.
After `make`, the script now records the result, reports build failure time when nonzero, and exits with the original make result at the end.
When `-d` is set, the script removes any existing `dump/`, recreates it, runs `python tools/parse_map.py`, and invokes `dadosod dol build/ssbm.us.1.2/main.dol -m build/map.csv -o dump`.
When both `-e` and `-d` are set and the build succeeds, the script syncs `dump/` into `expected/dump/` using `rsync -a --delete`.
Changed expected syncing so it only occurs when `expected=true` and the build result is zero.
smart_moves: Kept generated dump output out of version control by adding `dump/` to `.gitignore`.
Integrated dumping as an optional flag rather than changing the default build path, preserving normal build behavior.
Reused the existing `-e` expected-sync workflow so dump artifacts can be captured alongside expected build outputs when explicitly requested.
Deleted and recreated `dump/` before running `dadosod`, avoiding stale disassembly files from previous builds.
Preserved the make exit status by storing `result=$?` and ending with `exit "$result"`, so added post-build work does not hide the build result.
Regenerated map data with `tools/parse_map.py` immediately before `dadosod`, making the dump step depend on current build/map information.
decomp_lessons: Generated analysis or disassembly artifacts should usually live in ignored directories unless the PR explicitly intends to version them.
Build helper flags should be opt-in when they depend on extra local tooling such as Rust/cargo-installed utilities.
When adding post-build convenience steps, preserve the original build failure status so automation and shell users still get accurate success/failure behavior.
If generated artifacts can be part of an expected-output workflow, wire them into the same explicit sync flag instead of creating an unrelated mechanism.
Post-build tools that rely on maps should generate or refresh the map input close to the point of use to reduce stale-output confusion.
naming_conventions: The script uses lowercase boolean shell variables for feature flags, e.g. `expected`, `clear`, `log`, and the new `dump`.
Short build-script flags are documented in the usage block as `-x  name: explanation`; this PR follows that style with `-d  dump: ...`.
Generated dump output is named plainly as `dump/`, and expected synced dump output mirrors it as `expected/dump/`.
The dump command targets the existing build product path `build/ssbm.us.1.2/main.dol` and map CSV path `build/map.csv`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Adds a workflow hook for dumping the built `main.dol` back into assembly-like output using `dadosod`, which can support inspection during decompilation work.
Uses `tools/parse_map.py` before dumping so symbol/map information is available to `dadosod` through `-m build/map.csv`.
No direct code matching, asm edits, or symbol renaming tactics were present in the provided diff beyond enabling this dump-generation workflow.
review_feedback: The provided PR slice contains no issue comments or review comments.
There was one review recorded in counts, but no review body or actionable feedback was included in the available excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify the `build.sh` dump flag or replace `dadosod` usage.
Search for references to `expected/dump` to see whether dump artifacts became part of verification workflows.
Look for changes to `tools/parse_map.py` that affect `build/map.csv` compatibility with `dadosod`.
Check whether CI or documentation later mentions installing Rust or `dadosod` for decompilation workflows.
